freedomConfigAdaptation.description: Tenant-safe billing policy values that can change without altering subscription state ordering, payment-token handling, invoice idempotency, MRR normalization, or tenant isolation.

keys.semantic: How long plan publication audit records remain queryable for tenant administrators.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune audit retention by policy while plan OCC and store-before-emit ordering remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Default trial duration when a plan does not specify one explicitly.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may adapt trial posture without changing payment validation, duplicate-subscription prevention, or initial state rules.

keys.semantic: Retry cadence after a failed recurring charge.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may choose a gentler or stricter retry cadence while max attempts still derive from schedule length.

keys.semantic: Rolling window used by billing dashboards and analytics summaries.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune reporting windows while the additive-subtractive MRR formula remains MACHINE.

grammarAdaptation.description: FLOW-12 has no tenant-editable grammar surface. Tenants may tune billing policy values and presentation copy without changing payment or invoice event contracts.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: Tenant-user subscription actions, tenant-admin billing operations, platform-admin support, and public pricing visibility are fixed for certification. Tenant adaptation may not weaken role isolation or expose private billing records across tenants.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Tenant billing policies that can change while fixed plan publication, activation, recurring billing, and MRR invariants remain intact.

edgeCases.name: Longer enterprise trial
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can default plans to a longer trial for enterprise buyers.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T210 sets TRIALING when trialDays is greater than zero and ACTIVE when it is zero.

edgeCases.name: Gentle retry cadence
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can wait longer between failed charge attempts.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T211 reads the dunning schedule and derives maxAttempts from the schedule length.

edgeCases.name: Strict audit retention
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can retain plan-publication audit records longer for finance review.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T209 writes plan audit records before publishing plan events.

edgeCases.name: Quarterly billing dashboard
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can view billing metrics over a longer reporting window.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T212 stores tenant-level MRR metrics after recalculating active and trialing subscriptions.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenants cannot change without breaking portability or authorization certification.
rules: T209 rejects non-integer or non-positive priceCents before idempotency or writes.
T209 uses a tenant, plan, and version idempotency key before plan publication.
T209 stores plan audit records before enqueueing plan publication events.
T209 uses OCC for existing plan version writes.
T210 validates payment method before subscription state writes.
T210 stores paymentMethodId vault references only and never stores raw paymentMethodToken.
T210 prevents duplicate tenant, subscriber, and plan subscriptions with SETNX-style idempotency.
T211 checks subscription status before acquiring billing locks.
T211 acquires the billing lock before invoice generation and charge execution.
T211 derives invoiceId from tenant, subscription, and period key.
T211 stores billing audit records before enqueueing billing result events.
T211 derives maxAttempts from subscription_billing_dunning_schedule length.
T212 uses the fixed MRR normalization formula: MONTHLY equals priceCents, ANNUAL equals floor priceCents divided by 12, CUSTOM equals floor priceCents divided by intervalDays times 30.
T212 includes both SubscriptionActivated and SubscriptionCancelled paths.
T212 emits tenant-level metrics without subscriberId.
All FLOW-12 services remain FLOW_SCOPED and return DataProcessResult.

tenant-a-enterprise-billing.description: Tenant A uses a longer trial and gentler retry schedule for enterprise subscription buyers.
tenant-a-enterprise-billing.impact: Subscribers have more evaluation time and failed payments receive a slower recovery cadence while duplicate billing and payment-token privacy remain fixed.
tenant-a-enterprise-billing.testable: Tenant A adaptation evidence shows the policy in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory and fork CI still passes.

tenant-a-finance-review-mode.description: Tenant A extends audit retention and uses quarterly billing analytics.
tenant-a-finance-review-mode.impact: Finance teams retain plan audit evidence longer and view dashboard metrics over a broader reporting window without changing MRR math.
tenant-a-finance-review-mode.testable: Tenant A before/after assertion shows the adapted values in FREEDOM defaults without changing MACHINE invariants.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: Task type IDs T209 through T212

passCriteria: Tenant A can apply a TYPE-A billing policy change without altering MACHINE constraints.
The adapted policy is present in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory.
At least one before/after behavior assertion proves the adapted policy changes trial, dunning, audit, or metric-window behavior.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing token, 401 for cross-tenant token, and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
